marketState: neutral — extreme heat demand still accelerating offsets storage glut; seasonal bias bearish over 1-4 weeks
summary: Front-month NG at $3.279 is modestly above the current fair-value midpoint ($2.96) but inside the $2.46–$3.46 band, converging toward FAIR by day 21 — price is not at a valuation extreme. The dominant near-term factor is weather: gas-weighted demand is above the 5-year maximum in both the 1-7D (pctVs5y 59.5%, aboveMax) and 8-16D (pctVs5y 33.3%, aboveMax) windows, with the trend still accelerating (+4.2 in the past 24 hours, now running 44% above the 5-year normal) — a rare dual-window above-max event that provides real support for the next two weeks. The critical offset is the storage surplus: inventories sit +151 Bcf above the 5-year average and the most recent EIA report was a bearish surprise (+76 Bcf actual vs +70 Bcf forecast), confirming that record US production is absorbing the heat demand and preventing meaningful inventory draws. Seasonal odds are unfavorable over 1-4 weeks (only 19% probability of higher price at the 4-week horizon, typical move -1.8%, reliability graded Low), but managed money net shorts near a 5-year extreme (18th percentile) create latent short-squeeze risk if the heat event extends or surprises further.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonal odds are firmly against the long side over 3-4 weeks (19% probability of higher price in 4 weeks; typical move -1.8%; Low reliability with 51pp spread), and storage remains +151 Bcf above 5-year average with the last report surprising bearishly — record production is absorbing extreme demand, limiting the fundamental bullish case. The contrarian COT (MM net at 18th percentile short) and approaching seasonally strong September (+3.1% historical average) are structural positives, but the winter strip already prices in tightness (Jan 2027 at $4.43), leaving limited catalyst for a sustainable summer-fall rally above the $3.46 upper band.
longTerm.invalidation: A series of large weekly storage draws (100+ Bcf deficits vs 5-year average, cutting the surplus below ~50 Bcf) would flip the view to LONG; a daily close above $3.46 (upper fair-value band) sustained for 2+ sessions would trigger a SHORT re-entry setup.
longTerm.trigger: Enter SHORT if price reaches $3.44–$3.50 with storage surplus still elevated; enter LONG only if price falls to the $2.70–$2.90 zone with storage draws accelerating or a catalyst shifts the production outlook lower.

shortTerm.entry: $3.18–$3.22 (limit buy on intraday pullback; 5m/15m RSI ~34 and negative MACD signal a brief dip before potential resumption)
shortTerm.rationale: Both the 1-7D and 8-16D demand windows are above the 5-year maximum and the trend is accelerating (+4.2 in 24h, nowDemand 179.0 vs 5y normal +54.9), providing the strongest possible near-term demand backdrop. The 4h timeframe is constructively bullish (RSI 50.3, MACD positive, 50-EMA above 200-EMA), while 5m/15m RSI near 34 with negative MACD signals a brief tactical dip is underway — a pullback entry toward the $3.18–3.22 zone offers ~$0.15 risk to stop and ~$0.27 reward to the upper fair-value band at $3.45, with the COT short extreme (18th percentile) adding asymmetric squeeze risk to the upside.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $3.05 would indicate that the weather heat premium is being flushed out — likely triggered by a cooler weather revision in the 8-16D window or a bearish storage surprise larger than +10 Bcf vs estimate.

keyDrivers: Demand trend accelerating: +4.2 in 24h, nowDemand 179.0 running +44% above 5-year normal — both 1-7D and 8-16D windows above the 5-year maximum range
Storage structural headwind: +151 Bcf above 5-year average; last EIA report a bearish surprise (+6 Bcf vs estimate), confirming record production absorbs heat demand
COT managed-money net at 18th 5-year percentile (-82,722 net short, ratio 0.71) — historically crowded short creates short-squeeze tail risk
Seasonal 4-week probability of higher price only 19%; typical 4-week move -1.8% with Low reliability (51pp 10-90 spread) — structural seasonal headwind
Winter curve steeply backwardated (Jan 2027 at $4.43 vs $3.28 front, +$1.15); near-term Aug–Oct flat, implying market is not pricing summer scarcity at current levels
risks: Weather forecast cooling reversal: any moderation in the 8-16D window removes the primary short-term bull catalyst and exposes the full weight of the +151 Bcf storage overhang and bearish seasonal odds
Production resilience: US supply has already absorbed above-max heat demand (bearish storage surprise), meaning the weather rally may stall well below the $3.46 upper band without a production shock
COT short-squeeze spike: aggressive MM short-covering on hot weather headlines could drive a rapid spike above $3.46, creating stop-out risk before the seasonal bear trend reasserts
